params.name: order_by
params.description: Criteria for sorting results.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number. Value must be greater or equal to 1.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of results per page. Value must be between 1 and 100.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of Organization.

params.name: editable
params.description: Defines whether to filter out editable API keys or not.

params.name: expired
params.description: Defines whether to filter out expired API keys or not.

params.name: access_key
params.description: Filter by access key (deprecated in favor of `access_keys`).

params.name: description
params.description: Filter by description.

params.name: bearer_id
params.description: Filter by bearer ID.

params.name: bearer_type
params.description: Filter by type of bearer.

params.name: access_keys
params.description: Filter by a list of access keys.

items.docs: List API keys. By default, the API keys listed are ordered by creation date in ascending order. This can be modified via the `order_by` field. You can define additional parameters for your query such as `editable`, `expired`, `access_key` and `bearer_id`.

Create an API key. You must specify the `application_id` or the `user_id` and the description. You can also specify the `default_project_id`, which is the Project ID of your preferred Project, to use with Object Storage. The `access_key` and `secret_key` values are returned in the response. Note that the secret key is only shown once. Make sure that you copy and store both keys somewhere safe.

params.name: access_key
params.description: Access key to search for.

items.docs: Retrieve information about an API key, specified by the `access_key` parameter. The API key's details, including either the `user_id` or `application_id` of its bearer are returned in the response. Note that the string value for the `secret_key` is nullable, and therefore is not displayed in the response. The `secret_key` value is only displayed upon API key creation.

params.name: access_key
params.description: Access key to update.

items.docs: Update the parameters of an API key, including `default_project_id` and `description`.

params.name: access_key
params.description: Access key to delete.

items.docs: Delete an API key. Note that this action is irreversible and cannot be undone. Make sure you update any configurations using the API keys you delete.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Criteria for sorting results.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of results per page. Value must be between 1 and 100.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number. Value must be greater than 1.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the application to filter.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of the Organization to filter.

params.name: editable
params.description: Defines whether to filter out editable applications or not.

params.name: application_ids
params.description: Filter by list of IDs.

params.name: tag
params.description: Filter by tags containing a given string.

items.docs: List the applications of an Organization. By default, the applications listed are ordered by creation date in ascending order. This can be modified via the `order_by` field. You must define the `organization_id` in the query path of your request. You can also define additional parameters for your query such as `application_ids`.
Create a new application. You must define the `name` parameter in the request.

params.name: application_id
params.description: ID of the application to find.

items.docs: Retrieve information about an application, specified by the `application_id` parameter. The application's full details, including `id`, `email`, `organization_id`, `status` and `two_factor_enabled` are returned in the response.

params.name: application_id
params.description: ID of the application to update.

items.docs: Update the parameters of an application, including `name` and `description`.

params.name: application_id
params.description: ID of the application to delete.

items.docs: Delete an application. Note that this action is irreversible and will automatically delete the application's API keys. Policies attached to users and applications via this group will no longer apply.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of the Organization.

items.docs: Get your Organization's IAM information

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of the Organization.

items.docs: This will fail if an alias has already been defined. Please contact support if you need to change your Organization's alias.

info.name: Set your Organization's allowed login methods.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of the Organization.

items.docs: Set your Organization's allowed login methods.

params.description: ID of the user to list connections for.

params.name: user_id
params.description: ID of the user that will be added to your connection.

params.description: User ID.

params.name: user_id
params.description: ID of the user you want to manage the connection for.

items.docs: List the grace periods of a member.

params.description: Criteria for sorting results.

params.description: Number of results per page. Value must be between 1 and 100.

params.description: Page number. Value must be greater or equal to 1.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of the Organization to filter.

params.description: Filter by list of IDs.

params.name: mfa
params.description: Filter by MFA status.

params.name: tag
params.description: Filter by tags containing a given string.

params.name: type
params.description: Filter by user type.

items.docs: List the users of an Organization. By default, the users listed are ordered by creation date in ascending order. This can be modified via the `order_by` field. You must define the `organization_id` in the query path of your request. You can also define additional parameters for your query such as `user_ids`.

Create a new user. You must define the `organization_id` in your request. If you are adding a member, enter the member's details. If you are adding a guest, you must define the `email` and not add the member attribute.

params.name: user_id
params.description: ID of the user to find.

items.docs: Retrieve information about a user, specified by the `user_id` parameter. The user's full details, including `id`, `email`, `organization_id`, `status` and `mfa` are returned in the response.

params.name: user_id
params.description: ID of the user to update.

items.docs: Update the parameters of a user, including `tags`.

info.name: Delete a guest user from an Organization

params.name: user_id
params.description: ID of the user to delete.

items.docs: Remove a user from an Organization in which they are a guest. You must define the `user_id` in your request. Note that removing a user from an Organization automatically deletes their API keys, and any policies directly attached to them become orphaned.

params.name: user_id
params.description: ID of the user to lock.

items.docs: Lock a member. A locked member cannot log in or use API keys until the locked status is removed.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID of the MFA OTP.

items.docs: Create a MFA OTP.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID of the MFA OTP.

items.docs: Delete a MFA OTP.

params.name: user_id
params.description: ID of the user to unlock.

items.docs: Unlock a member

params.description: ID of the user to update.

items.docs: Update an user's password.

params.description: ID of the user to update.

items.docs: Update an user's username.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID of the MFA OTP.

items.docs: Validate a MFA OTP.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Sort order of groups.

params.name: page
params.description: Requested page number. Value must be greater or equal to 1.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of items per page. Value must be between 1 and 100.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: Filter by Organization ID.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of group to find.

params.name: application_ids
params.description: Filter by a list of application IDs.

params.name: user_ids
params.description: Filter by a list of user IDs.

params.name: group_ids
params.description: Filter by a list of group IDs.

params.name: tag
params.description: Filter by tags containing a given string.

items.docs: List groups. By default, the groups listed are ordered by creation date in ascending order. This can be modified via the `order_by` field. You can define additional parameters to filter your query. Use `user_ids` or `application_ids` to list all groups certain users or applications belong to.
Create a new group. You must define the `name` and `organization_id` parameters in the request.

params.name: group_id
params.description: ID of the group.

items.docs: Retrieve information about a given group, specified by the `group_id` parameter. The group's full details, including `user_ids` and `application_ids` are returned in the response.

params.name: group_id
params.description: ID of the group to update.

items.docs: Update the parameters of group, including `name` and `description`.

params.name: group_id
params.description: ID of the group to delete.

items.docs: Delete a group. Note that this action is irreversible and could delete permissions for group members. Policies attached to users and applications via this group will no longer apply.

info.name: Add a user or an application to a group

params.name: group_id
params.description: ID of the group.

items.docs: Add a user or an application to a group. You can specify a `user_id` and `application_id` in the body of your request. Note that you can only add one of each per request.

info.name: Add multiple users and applications to a group

params.name: group_id
params.description: ID of the group.

items.docs: Add multiple users and applications to a group in a single call. You can specify an array of `user_id`s and `application_id`s. Note that any existing users and applications in the group will remain. To add new users/applications and delete pre-existing ones, use the [Overwrite users and applications of a group](#path-groups-overwrite-users-and-applications-of-a-group) method.

info.name: Overwrite users and applications of a group

items.docs: Overwrite users and applications configuration in a group. Any information that you add using this command will overwrite the previous configuration.

info.name: Remove a user or an application from a group

params.name: group_id
params.description: ID of the group.

items.docs: Remove a user or an application from a group. You can specify a `user_id` and `application_id` in the body of your request. Note that you can only remove one of each per request. Removing a user from a group means that any permissions given to them via the group (i.e. from an attached policy) will no longer apply. Be sure you want to remove these permissions from the user before proceeding.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Criteria for sorting results.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of results per page. Value must be between 1 and 100.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number. Value must be greater than 1.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of the Organization to filter.

params.name: editable
params.description: Defines whether or not filter out editable policies.

params.name: user_ids
params.description: Defines whether or not to filter by list of user IDs.

params.name: group_ids
params.description: Defines whether or not to filter by list of group IDs.

params.name: application_ids
params.description: Filter by a list of application IDs.

params.name: no_principal
params.description: Defines whether or not the policy is attributed to a principal.

params.name: policy_name
params.description: Name of the policy to fetch.

params.name: tag
params.description: Filter by tags containing a given string.

params.name: policy_ids
params.description: Filter by a list of IDs.

items.docs: List the policies of an Organization. By default, the policies listed are ordered by creation date in ascending order. This can be modified via the `order_by` field. You must define the `organization_id` in the query path of your request. You can also define additional parameters to filter your query, such as `user_ids`, `groups_ids`, `application_ids`, and `policy_name`.

Create a new application. You must define the `name` parameter in the request. You can specify parameters such as `user_id`, `groups_id`, `application_id`, `no_principal`, `rules` and its child attributes.

params.name: policy_id
params.description: Id of policy to search.

items.docs: Retrieve information about a policy, specified by the `policy_id` parameter. The policy's full details, including `id`, `name`, `organization_id`, `nb_rules` and `nb_scopes`, `nb_permission_sets` are returned in the response.

params.name: policy_id
params.description: Id of policy to update.

items.docs: Update the parameters of a policy, including `name`, `description`, `user_id`, `group_id`, `application_id` and `no_principal`.

params.name: policy_id
params.description: Id of policy to delete.

items.docs: Delete a policy. You must define specify the `policy_id` parameter in your request. Note that when deleting a policy, all permissions it gives to its principal (user, group or application) will be revoked.
Clone a policy. You must define specify the `policy_id` parameter in your request.

info.name: List all quotas in the Organization

params.name: order_by
params.description: Criteria for sorting results.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of results per page. Value must be between 1 and 100.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number. Value must be greater than 1.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: Filter by Organization ID.

params.name: quotum_names
params.description: List of quotum names to filter from.

items.docs: List all product and features quota for an Organization, with their associated limits. By default, the quota listed are ordered by creation date in ascending order. This can be modified via the `order_by` field. You must define the `organization_id` in the query path of your request.

info.name: Get a quota in the Organization

params.name: quotum_name
params.description: Name of the quota to get.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of the Organization.

items.docs: Retrieve information about a resource quota, specified by the `quotum_name` parameter. The quota's `limit`, or whether it is unlimited, is returned in the response.

info.name: List rules of a given policy

params.name: policy_id
params.description: Id of policy to search.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of results per page. Value must be between 1 and 100.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number. Value must be greater than 1.

items.docs: List the rules of a given policy. By default, the rules listed are ordered by creation date in ascending order. This can be modified via the `order_by` field. You must define the `policy_id` in the query path of your request.

info.name: Set rules of a given policy

items.docs: Overwrite the rules of a given policy. Any information that you add using this command will overwrite the previous configuration. If you include some of the rules you already had in your previous configuration in your new one, but you change their order, the new order of display will apply. While policy rules are ordered, they have no impact on the access logic of IAM because rules are allow-only.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Sort order of the SSH keys.

params.name: page
params.description: Requested page number. Value must be greater or equal to 1.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of items per page. Value must be between 1 and 100.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: Filter by Organization ID.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of group to find.

params.name: project_id
params.description: Filter by Project ID.

params.name: disabled
params.description: Defines whether to include disabled SSH keys or not.

items.docs: List SSH keys. By default, the SSH keys listed are ordered by creation date in ascending order. This can be modified via the `order_by` field. You can define additional parameters for your query such as `organization_id`, `name`, `project_id` and `disabled`.
Add a new SSH key to a Scaleway Project. You must specify the `name`, `public_key` and `project_id`.

params.name: ssh_key_id
params.description: ID of the SSH key.

items.docs: Retrieve information about a given SSH key, specified by the `ssh_key_id` parameter. The SSH key's full details, including `id`, `name`, `public_key`, and `project_id` are returned in the response.
Update the parameters of an SSH key, including `name` and `disable`.
Delete a given SSH key, specified by the `ssh_key_id`. Deleting an SSH is permanent, and cannot be undone. Note that you might need to update any configurations that used the SSH key.
